title: Brian Allen Lipson
content:
People.ContactEmail blipson@mccarthy.ca
- British Columbia, 2024
Brian is a partner and co-leader of the firm’s national Transportation and Logistics group.
Licensed to practise in Quebec since 2014 and in British Columbia since 2024, Brian leverages McCarthy Tétrault’s unique national platform to provide strategic advice to clients worldwide whose businesses intersect with the supply chain in Canada. His hybrid business and litigation practice includes mergers and acquisitions, all manner of commercial agreements, regulatory compliance, and licensing in all modes of surface and air transportation and warehousing, with a particular interest in structuring transactions and reorganizations in order to minimize impact to permits and registration.
Brian has provided advice in respect of numerous Canadian and cross-border purchase and sale agreements, reorganizations, and investments. He regularly acts for non-Canadian acquirers of warehouses, rail assets and aircraft, and assists them in negotiating regulatory hurdles and in understanding the Canadian market. He offers contractual solutions and advice on liability and risk exposure to warehousemen and storers, to rail carriers and owners of rail rolling stock, to motor carriers and brokers of freight, to shippers and beneficial cargo owners in all modes, to governments and municipalities, and to providers of public transportation, including in the areas of autonomous vehicles and Remotely Piloted Aircraft Systems (drones).
Brian has extensive experience with licensing and permitting requirements for commercial motor vehicles and for automotive manufacturers and dealers throughout Canada, including obtaining regulatory consents and exemptions from Transport Canada under the Motor Vehicle Safety Act as well as licences from the Ontario Motor Vehicle Industry Council (OMVIC) and other similar regulators in the context of cross-border acquisitions and expansions.
Brian regularly represents rail carriers in respect of administrative monetary penalties before the Transportation Appeal Tribunal of Canada, and represents both shippers and carriers in respect of all manner of disputes before the Canadian Transportation Agency.

#### Brian’s recent experience includes advising:
•	Canadian National Railway Company in respect of freight agreements, regulatory compliance, access to information requests, and disputes before the Transportation Appeal Tribunal of Canada, the Canadian Transportation Agency, and the Federal Courts of Canada. •	National Bank of Canada in its $5B acquisition of Canadian Western Bank, including a motor vehicle leasing division comprising over 10,000 vehicles. •	Gogo Inc., a leader in the global business aviation market, in its acquisition of Satcom Direct, Inc. •	Blue Wolf Capital Partners LLC in its $1.2B acquisition of LOGISTEC Corporation. •	Via Rail Canada Inc. in connection with matters before the Canadian Transportation Agency.
Brian obtained a Bachelor of Laws (LL.B.) degree from Université Laval in 2013 and was called to the Quebec Bar in 2014. He also received, in 2001, a Bachelor of Arts (B.A.) in Music Composition and Improvisation from the University of Michigan (Ann Arbor), and has been working as a composer and trumpet player since 1999. His original score for the play The Bacchae received the 2013 Montreal English Theatre Award for Outstanding Sound Design.
Brian is a member of the Canadian Bar Association, the Canadian Transport Lawyers Association, and the (U.S.) Transportation Lawyers Association.
